# Last Time

::: {.incremental}
+ **Binary Search**
+ **std::array**
+ **Passing Arrays, std::array, and Vectors to Functions**
+ **Parallel Arrays**
:::

::: notes
12.01 recap. Binary search: O(log N), requires sorted array, low/high/mid pattern. std::array: fixed-size, no pointer decay, .size() and .at(). Three-type comparison: int[] vs array<T,N> vs vector. Passing: int[] decays (pass size separately), std::array and vector by const ref. Parallel arrays: shared index, same SIZE constant.
:::

---

# Today's Agenda

- Two-Dimensional Arrays
- Arrays with Three or More Dimensions
- Lab — Array-Backed Stack

::: notes

:::

{{< include topic_1_two_dimensional.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_2_three_dimensions.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_3_stack_lab.qmd >}}

---

# Key Takeaways

- Declare 2D arrays as `type name[rows][cols]`; traverse with nested loops, outer on rows
- Row-major storage means row-by-row traversal is faster than column-by-column
- A stack API (push/pop/peek/isFull/isEmpty) built on an array is a first example of separating behavior from storage

---

# Next Time

---

# Appendix

---

## Two-Dimensional Arrays: Good Practices

---

## Two-Dimensional Arrays: Common Mistakes

---

## Two-Dimensional Arrays: More Examples

---

## Two-Dimensional Arrays: Practice Problems

---

## Two-Dimensional Arrays: AI Search Terms

---

## Two-Dimensional Arrays: Questions for Reflection

---

## Arrays with Three or More Dimensions: Good Practices

---

## Arrays with Three or More Dimensions: Common Mistakes

---

## Arrays with Three or More Dimensions: More Examples

---

## Arrays with Three or More Dimensions: Practice Problems

---

## Arrays with Three or More Dimensions: AI Search Terms

---

## Arrays with Three or More Dimensions: Questions for Reflection

---

## Lab — Array-Backed Stack: Good Practices

---

## Lab — Array-Backed Stack: Common Mistakes

---

## Lab — Array-Backed Stack: More Examples

---

## Lab — Array-Backed Stack: Practice Problems

---

## Lab — Array-Backed Stack: AI Search Terms

---

## Lab — Array-Backed Stack: Questions for Reflection
